title: Aureline provider-entry browser-handoff packet seed: provider-side typed packet, return summary, and packet audit-event vocabulary
description: Provider-side boundary schema for the typed browser-handoff packet seed minted by an entry on the connected-account / provider registry. The packet pins origin, target, expected actor scope, destination class, reason code, freshness window, mutation disposition, and (when applicable) the publish-later object seed the handoff defers to, so a provider-linked entry path never reduces to a generic 'Open in browser' affordance. This schema composes with — and never forks — the canonical /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json packet record and the canonical /schemas/providers/browser_handoff_sheet.schema.json sheet record; it adds the registry-side wrapper the M1 entry path mints. Three record kinds are exported: provider_entry_browser_handoff_packet_record (the minted packet seed), provider_entry_browser_handoff_return_record (the typed return summary after a callback resolves), and provider_entry_browser_handoff_audit_event_record (an observable event on the provider_handoff stream the packet seed generates). Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs and structured fields only. Adding a new packet_audit_event_id, packet_state_class, or return_outcome_class is additive-minor and requires a provider_entry_browser_handoff_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

provider_entry_browser_handoff_schema_ve.description: Integer schema version for this provider-entry browser-handoff packet seed export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show on desktop, CLI, notifications, provider-linked headers, docs/help, support exports, and audit rollups. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

destination_class.description: Frozen destination-class vocabulary reused verbatim from /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. external_generic_web is repair-only and MUST pin reason_code = external_docs_or_runbook on this seed.

reason_code.description: Frozen reason-for-handoff vocabulary reused verbatim from /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Every packet seed names exactly one; a generic 'Open in browser' affordance without a typed reason is forbidden.

provider_actor_class.description: Frozen provider-actor class vocabulary reused verbatim from /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden; 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only.

mutation_disposition_class.description: Frozen mutation disposition reused verbatim from /schemas/providers/connected_account_registry.schema.json. A packet seed minted on a provider-linked entry path MUST carry browser_handoff_required or publish_later_required; immediate_mutation_in_product and inspect_only_no_mutation entries do not mint a browser-handoff packet seed.

freshness_class.description: Frozen freshness class reused verbatim from /schemas/providers/connected_account_registry.schema.json.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the packet seed was minted under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0007 and ADR-0010 redaction posture.

packet_state_class.description: Frozen packet-state vocabulary. ready_for_user_confirmation is the initial state. launched_awaiting_callback is the in-flight state. callback_validated and callback_rejected mirror the underlying integration packet's outcome. Silent transition between states is forbidden.

return_outcome_class.description: Frozen return-outcome class. The return record names exactly one; silent collapse into a generic 'closed' state is forbidden.

packet_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids the provider-entry browser-handoff packet seed fires on the ADR-0010 provider_handoff stream. Mirrors the underlying integration-level browser-handoff-packet audit events; the provider-entry layer carries the registry-side intent the integration packet does not (which connected-account registry entry minted the packet).

object_class.description: Provider-side object class the packet seed targets. Aligned with the connected-account registry and publish-later seed vocabularies.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the packet seed was minted under. An inbound callback whose origin does not match is denied with callback_rejected.

target_disclosure.description: Canonical target the packet seed routes to. Names destination_class, canonical_host, tenant_or_org_scope, object_class, and provider_side_id; raw URLs and raw query strings are forbidden.

anchor_kind.description: Frozen return-anchor kind reused verbatim from /schemas/providers/browser_handoff_sheet.schema.json.

reentry_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence explaining why the user is re-entering product scope and where they land. A generic 'returned' summary is forbidden.

return_summary.description: Stable anchor plus a reviewable sentence so the return into product scope is itself explicit. Round-trips independently of whether the remote system preserves query strings.

freshness_window.description: Freshness window the packet seed was observed under. Surfaces MUST render the freshness class; never_observed packet seeds MUST NOT claim a healthy observation.

connected_account_registry_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref to the connected_account_registry_entry_record that minted this packet seed. Required so the registry side of the handoff is inspectable from the packet.

provider_descriptor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider_descriptor_record the entry points at. The descriptor's supported_capabilities MUST include supports_browser_handoff or supports_publish_later; otherwise the seed is refused at mint.

integration_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the underlying browser_handoff_packet_record exported by /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json once one has been minted. Empty string only on seeds that have not yet bound to a live integration packet (state ready_for_user_confirmation before user confirm).

publish_later_object_seed_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publish_later_object_seed_record this handoff defers to. Required when mutation_disposition_class is publish_later_required; empty string when mutation_disposition_class is browser_handoff_required and the action is not deferred.

reason_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence quoting the reason code in human-legible form. Surfaces render this verbatim; flattening it into a generic 'External link' is forbidden.

departure_disclosure_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence explaining why the user is leaving product scope and what privacy / data-loss consequence applies. A packet seed without a departure disclosure is refused at mint.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket record admitting the mutation. Required when reason_code is mutation-class (every reason except provider_consent_flow); empty string for provider_consent_flow.

replay_expires_at.description: Monotonic timestamp after which the packet seed is no longer admissible. The seed's user-confirm action MUST refuse after this time and re-mint a fresh seed.

allOf.description: A packet seed minted on a provider-linked entry path MUST carry browser_handoff_required or publish_later_required.
publish_later_required seeds MUST cite a publish_later_object_seed_ref.
external_generic_web destinations MUST pin reason_code = external_docs_or_runbook.
Mutation-class reason codes MUST cite an approval_ticket_ref; provider_consent_flow MAY omit one.
expected_authority_on_destination unknown_actor_class MUST NOT advance past ready_for_user_confirmation.

provider_entry_browser_handoff_packet_re.description: Provider-entry browser-handoff packet seed minted by a connected_account_registry_entry. Names origin, target, expected actor scope, freshness, departure disclosure, return summary, and (when applicable) the publish-later object seed the handoff defers to. Composes with — does not redefine — the canonical /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json packet record.

connected_account_registry_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref to the registry entry the return resolves against. Empty string only for synthetic return records from a repair path.

return_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence naming what was completed (or denied) and where the user re-enters product scope. A generic 'returned' summary is forbidden.

completed_publish_later_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publish_later_queue_item_record admitted on completed_publish_later_admitted; empty string otherwise.

denial_reason_summary.description: Optional sentence on denied_* outcomes naming the denial cause without raw provider payloads.

provider_entry_browser_handoff_return_re.description: Typed return summary for a provider-entry browser-handoff packet seed once the user has returned to product scope. Names the outcome class explicitly so the return is never rendered as a generic 'closed' state on docs/help, support exports, or audit rollups.

connected_account_registry_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref to the registry entry the packet seed was minted from.

integration_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the underlying /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json record once minted.

provider_entry_browser_handoff_audit_eve.description: An observable event on the ADR-0010 provider_handoff audit stream that captures registry-side intent the integration-level browser-handoff-packet audit events cannot (which registry entry minted the packet seed, which descriptor it pointed at). Raw URLs, raw tokens, and raw callback bodies are forbidden on every audit-event record.
